description: 8-turn conversation establishing a process preference (handling breaking changes). Tests recall of an instruction-category memory.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Our rule for breaking changes: never ship one without a deprecation window of at least one minor release.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Understood — breaking changes require a deprecation window of at least one minor release before removal.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Can you review this PR that renames the auth endpoint?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: This renames a public endpoint — that's a breaking change. Per your rule it needs a deprecation window of at least one minor release.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Right, let's alias the old path for one release first.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Good — I'll keep the old path as an alias for one minor release, then remove it.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Remind me how we handle breaking changes.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Never ship a breaking change without a deprecation window of at least one minor release.

gold_answers.breaking_change_policy: Deprecation window of at least one minor release before removal
